title: Record M3 ordinary life architecture gate
summary: Record the M3 architecture decision for ordinary-life state ownership, derived indexes, jobs, save/replay, Worker parity and performance before implementation.
acceptance: ADR documents alternatives chosen boundaries migration rollback test and performance implications for M3 ordinary-life simulation
Owner stores are named for needs rest food health conditions abilities medical care mood thoughts memories relationships day/night and weather
Derived indexes dirty queues candidate caps Top-K limits versioned invalidation and structured ReasonTrace ownership are specified
Serializable job-driver boundaries are specified for rest eating and treatment jobs
Block conditions are explicit for owner-store gaps global scans unversioned caches public Worker protocol save/schema changes new dependencies package-boundary exceptions UI authority and M4 scope
No runtime implementation package manifests lockfiles schema protocol save format or dependency changes are included

history.details: Created by WM-0045 M3 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Create the reviewed M3 ADR gate so implementation cannot begin with ambiguous state ownership or hidden authority changes.
forbiddenPaths: M4 lamp Chronicle anomaly crisis-chain or director implementation
benchmarkImpact: Names M3 metric surfaces and regression gates without adding artifacts or updating baselines.
reviewRouting: Systems-architect completes to reviewer. If rejected, all downstream M3 implementation tasks remain proposed or blocked.
rollbackModel: Revert ADR and doc/report edits; downstream implementation remains proposed and M3 stays unstarted.
ownerGate: Document alternatives rather than assuming the design
Reject hidden shared state and UI-owned authority
Name owner store for every mutable fact before downstream coding
Block if any implementation would need public protocol save schema dependency or package-boundary change outside this ADR

sparkEligibility.reason: Architecture, state ownership, save/replay, Worker parity and performance boundary decisions are outside Spark scope.
